questions.question: Do you consciously step away from your phone before going to bed?
How soon before sleep do you usually put your phone to bed?
Where does your phone sleep at night?

options.label: I have a special designated place away from my bed

questions.question: Do you follow a ritual away from screens such as reading, meditation, that helps you drift off?
What’s the first thing you touch in the morning?
How soon after waking do you let the outside world in through your phone?
Do you start the day with a ritual—however small—before reaching for your phone?
Do you feel what you take in first thing through screens can affect your mood?
In your closest relationships, do you feel that phones impact the time you spend together?
In a world of relentless distraction, do any of the following resonate?

options.label: My body feels a subtle tension that lingers
My brain feels like it is slowly shrinking
I watch things that aren’t relevant or helpful to my life
I sense that others are distracted in my company
It stops me from doing things I’d like to be doing
No, my phone only has a positive impact in my life

questions.question: If you chose to create more space between you and your phone—even briefly— how might your life be better? (Select all that apply)

options.label: My sleep would be less disturbed
I would have more energy in my body
I might feel more focus and clarity
A sense of pride in myself
Less urgency or pressure to respond or act
I’d pay attention to how I actually feel
Attentive listening to the people that count
It would inspire me to be more spontaneous
By looking up, I’d notice more

questions.question: If you had ten more minutes away from your phone each day, what might you give that time to?
Can you tell us your age? (optional)
Can you tell us your gender? (optional)
